title: Committee sues former GM over corporate theft of more than $1M: lawsuit
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Pier 50, LLC is suing former GM Demetri Tsolakis for alleged corporate theft of over $1 million from Committee.
The lawsuit claims Tsolakis misused funds for personal expenses and his own restaurants, filing in Suffolk County Superior Court.
Tsolakis' attorney disputes the allegations, questioning his financial control and citing overlapping vendors as industry standard.

title: Roman Anthony calls himself out for ‘bad baseball’ after latest poor throw
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony's poor throws contribute to Boston Red Sox's 2-8 start in the 2026 season.
Anthony's weak throw in the 8th inning led to two Milwaukee Brewers runs, resulting in an 8-6 Red Sox loss.
Manager Alex Cora suggests mechanical issues as the cause, with possible future appearances at DH for Anthony.

title: Red Sox’ Willson Contreras warns Brewers he’ll ‘take one of them out’ if he’s hit by another pitch ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Willson Contreras criticized the Milwaukee Brewers after being hit by a pitch for the 24th time in his career.
Contreras, frustrated by being repeatedly hit, warned he might retaliate if he's hit again by Brewers pitchers.
Red Sox lost 8-6 to Milwaukee, with Contreras reaching base five times, including a solo homer in the ninth inning.

title: Worcester man receives life sentence for home invasion
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Concepcion Rodriguez, 39, sentenced to life in prison for a 2022 home invasion in Fitchburg.
Rodriguez faces added prison time for eight charges including armed assault and firearm possession.
Rodriguez's attorney, Kevin Larson, plans to appeal, citing wrongful conviction and Eighth Amendment concerns.

title: Derrick White talks beer, being a Bostonian, and a promising Celtics playoff run
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White participates in Samuel Adams' event for new beer line honoring Boston sports stars.
Celtics, second in Eastern Conference with a 53-25 record, eye another title with Tatum's return.
White praises Coach Joe Mazzulla for keeping team ready and exceeding expectations this season.

title: Mike Krzyzewski threw a subtle jab at Bill Belichick in response to new UNC hire
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Mike Krzyzewski commented on UNC's hire of Michael Malone as men's basketball coach, noting Duke's recent football win over UNC.
UNC hired former NBA coach Michael Malone, replacing Hubert Davis, while Bill Belichick is head football coach after Mack Brown's firing.
Krzyzewski noted the learning curve from pro to college level, mentioning UNC's attempt to model their programs after professional sports.

title: Person stabs dog in Milton during alleged attack
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A person stabbed a dog during an alleged attack in Milton on Blue Hill River Road, State Police reported.
The dog, attacked by the person, was taken to a local veterinary clinic for treatment.
Milton Animal Control is currently investigating the incident involving the off-leash dog and the individual.

title: Alex Cora reshuffles Red Sox lineup, drops Trevor Story out of No. 2 spot
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora moved Trevor Story to No. 5 in the lineup due to a .119 batting average.
Masataka Yoshida will bat second with Roman Anthony leading off, and Jarren Duran will be benched Monday.
Boston scores 3.3 runs per game, with a 27.8% strikeout rate, ranking fifth highest in MLB.

title: Cleveland radio host says Cavaliers are ‘scared’ of facing the Celtics in the playoffs
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cleveland Cavaliers aim to avoid a second-round playoff matchup with Boston Celtics, preferring to stay in the No. 4 seed.
Cavaliers' past playoff exits include being beaten by the Celtics in the 2024 second round.
Cleveland struggles with lineup decisions and injuries as the regular season concludes, complicating their playoff strategy.

title: Where you live in Mass. can shape your electric bill — is yours getting out of control?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts has the highest electricity costs in the continental U.S., with significant variations based on utility type.
Customers in communities with municipal utilities often pay about half as much for electricity compared to those with investor-owned utilities.
Switching to municipal utilities can be costly, but municipal aggregation offers a potential alternative for managing electricity costs.
